fields.label: API key
fields.description: PagerDuty REST API Key (prefer generating read-only key)

relationships.description: PagerDuty API key authentication: the auth scheme discriminator (jsonData.auth.id = 'api_key') selects the security scheme, and the REST API key is stored under secureJsonData['auth.api_key.apiKey'] — the secret key name embeds the selected scheme id.

instructions.msg: PagerDuty supports a single authentication method: a PagerDuty REST API key (an OpenAPI 'apiKey' security scheme). No-auth is not supported (pkg/customization.json:5). The config editor auto-selects the scheme and stores it as jsonData.auth.id='api_key'; you must set this exact value when provisioning.

Minimal working payload: {"jsonData":{"auth":{"id":"api_key"}},"secureJsonData":{"auth.api_key.apiKey":"<your-pagerduty-api-token>"}}. The secret is stored under the literal, dotted key name 'auth.api_key.apiKey' (secrets are namespaced by scheme id: auth.<schemeId>.apiKey). This matches the official provisioning example (docs/sources/_index.md:88-99).

Enter the raw PagerDuty REST API key only. The backend prepends the 'Token token=' prefix and sends it in the 'Authorization' header — i.e. 'Authorization: Token token=<key>' (pkg/openapids/httpclient.go:42-48; header name from pkg/spec.json:3700-3705; prefix from pkg/customization.json:9). Do not include the 'Token token=' prefix yourself; the backend adds it if missing. Prefer a read-only key (docs/sources/_index.md:47).

secureJsonData['auth.api_key.apiKey'] is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the key is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields['auth.api_key.apiKey'] (boolean) instead of secureJsonData.

There is no URL or host setting. The base URL is fixed to https://api.pagerduty.com by the bundled OpenAPI spec (pkg/spec.json:164-169), which defines exactly one server and no server variables, so the editor renders no connection section (src/openapids/components/config-editor/Connection.tsx:43-45) and jsonData.servers is unused. The plugin targets the US PagerDuty region only; the EU region (api.eu.pagerduty.com) is not selectable.

Health check: Save & test issues GET https://api.pagerduty.com/incidents (pkg/customization.json:18 marks it healthcheck:true). A missing or invalid API key returns HTTP 401 and the health check fails; success reports 'PagerDuty API datasource connected successfully' (pkg/openapids/plugin.go:80-89).
